title: Global Challenges Research Fund: value for money assessment
body: "This study is part of the 5-year evaluation of GCRF (2020 to 2025) that aims to assess whether GCRF’s realised outputs and achievements are in line with its expected outcomes.   Year 4 value for money (VfM) assessment of GCRF (published, May 2025). Year 5 value for money (VfM): evaluation of the GCRF (published, September 2025).   The study assesses the performance of 50 GCRF awards using an innovative, rubric-based instrument. This assessment rubric includes 3 components:   characteristics of the award dimensions and subdimensions performance standards, rated on a five-point scale    Both reports were produced in 2024. The Global Challenges Research Fund (GCRF) has now closed. Since then, the government has taken the difficult decision to temporarily reduce Official Development Assistance (ODA) to the equivalent of 0.3% of gross national income (GNI) by 2027 to fund an increase in defence spending. The government remains committed to international development and to returning ODA to 0.7% of GNI when fiscal conditions allow.   "
